trivia: The Super 80 stretched the DC-9 into the deregulation era's favorite jet. American Airlines alone flew hundreds. Today a hardy few soldier on with charter and cargo operators, mostly in the Americas.
The long, impossibly slim body with tiny tail-mounted engines earned the family its nickname: the Long Beach pencil.
funFacts: American Airlines operated over 360 MD-80s at peak.
Its five-abreast cabin was a passenger favorite.
